SOUTH ORANGE COUNTY LEADERSHIP CONFERENCE
POST OFFTCE BOX 3178 . MTSSTON V|EJO CA 92690 . Aq 45f-o4'19
ItuJZ ga ltt,fi
May 29, 1990
Mr. Johnathan Rothrnan
Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
P. O. Box 807
sacramento, california 
9 58 04-08 07
Dear Mr. Rothnan:
on the morning of May 16th, f contacted you to ask your assj-stance
in deternining whether our 501.3(c) non-profit association, the
south orange county Leadership conference, lrou1d be considered as
a public agency, and therefore, subject to restrictions 
under
Proposition 73, particularly as it relates to mass nailings. After
research into the question, you phoned me back later in the day
with the conclusions that we were not bound by the provisions of
Proposition 73, as we did not meet all the criteria 
for public
agencies established by the proposition.
Thank you for the effort you put forth on our behalf, while doing
research on this topic. I would appreciate the reiteration of your
conclusions in writing, so we may have a copy of your decision on
record in our files.
Thank you again for your assistance. Please rnail your repfy to:
CaroLyn wallace, South Orange county Leadership conference, P. o.
Box 3178, Mission Viejo, Cafifornia 92690. If you should need to
contact me, I nay be reached at (71-4) 458-04L9.
Cordially,
QtteUVn R tLta-llau /!lL
Carolyn K. Wallace 
U
Executive Coordinator
